concepts.title: Integrals of some special forms
concepts.conceptBody:
Integrals of some special forms

When integrand consists of even powers of $\text{x}$ only

Q) Integrate $\frac{\left( {{x}^{2}}+1 \right)\left( {{x}^{2}}+2 \right)}{\left( {{x}^{2}}+3 \right)\left( {{x}^{2}}+4 \right)}$ 

Solution: 

For these kind of question, we can put ${{\text{x}}^{2}}=\text{t}$ for making partial fractions $\And $ then put the value back for finding integrand.

$\frac{\left( {{x}^{2}}+1 \right)\left( {{x}^{2}}+2 \right)}{\left( {{x}^{2}}+3 \right)\left( {{x}^{2}}+4 \right)}\overset{{{x}^{2}}=t}{\mathop{\to }}\,\frac{\left( t+1 \right)\left( t+2 \right)}{\left( t+3 \right)\left( t+4 \right)}$

since the term is not in proper rational form, we will first convert it into that.

$1+\frac{\left( t+1 \right)\left( t+2 \right)}{\left( t+3 \right)\left( t+4 \right)}-1$

$~=1+\frac{\left( t+1 \right)\left( t+2 \right)-\left( t+3 \right)\left( t+4 \right)}{\left( t+3 \right)\left( t+4 \right)}$

now degree of denominator is greater than numerator making it a proper fraction (as ${{\text{t}}^{2}}$ term will cancel out).

$=1+\frac{\left( -4t-10 \right)}{\left( t+3 \right)\left( t+4 \right)}\to \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ now }\!\!~\!\!\text{ using }\!\!~\!\!\text{ partial }\!\!~\!\!\text{ fraction }\!\!~\!\!\text{ for }\!\!~\!\!\text{ this }\!\!~\!\!\text{ part}\text{. }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$

Let, $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{I}_{1}}=\frac{4t+10}{\left( t+3 \right)\left( t+4 \right)}=2\frac{2t+5}{\left( t+3 \right)\left( t+4 \right)}dt$

$\frac{2t+5}{\left( t+3 \right)\left( t+4 \right)}=\frac{A}{t+3}+\frac{B}{t+4}$

$\text{A}={{\left. \frac{2\text{t}+5}{\text{t}+4} \right|}_{\text{t}=-3}}=-1$

$\text{B}={{\left. \frac{2\text{t}+5}{\text{t}+3} \right|}_{\text{t}=-4}}=3$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Net function becomes

$1+\frac{2}{t+3}-\frac{6}{t+3}$

putting back the value of $t$

$=1+\frac{2}{{{x}^{2}}+3}-\frac{6}{{{x}^{2}}+4}$

integrating it

I $~=\int ~dx+2\int ~\frac{dt}{{{x}^{2}}+3}-6\int ~\frac{dt}{{{x}^{2}}+4}$

$~=x+\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{x}{\sqrt{3}}-\frac{6}{2}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{x}{2}+C$

Some Special integrals 

Set-I $1.\int \frac{dx}{{{x}^{2}}-{{a}^{2}}}=\frac{1}{2a}\text{log}\left| \frac{x-a}{x+a} \right|+c\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ 2. $\int \frac{dx}{{{a}^{2}}-{{x}^{2}}}=\frac{1}{2a}\text{log}\left| \frac{a+x}{a-x} \right|+c$ 

3.$\int \frac{dx}{{{x}^{2}}+{{a}^{2}}}=\frac{1}{a}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{x}{a}+c$ 
 

Q) (i) $\int \frac{dx}{1+4{{x}^{2}}}$ (ii) $\int \frac{dx}{1-25{{x}^{2}}}$ (iii) $\int \frac{\text{dx}}{9{{\text{x}}^{2}}-1}$ 

Solution: 

(i) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ I}=\int \frac{\text{dx}}{1+4{{\text{x}}^{2}}}=\frac{1}{4}\int \frac{\text{dx}}{\frac{1}{4}+{{\text{x}}^{2}}}$

$=\frac{1}{4}\int ~\frac{\text{dx}}{{{\left( \frac{1}{2} \right)}^{2}}+{{\text{x}}^{2}}}=\frac{1}{4}\cdot \frac{1}{\frac{1}{2}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{\text{x}}{\frac{1}{2}} \right)+\text{c}=\frac{1}{2}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( 2\text{x} \right)+\text{c}$

(ii) $\text{I}=\int \frac{\text{dx}}{1-25{{\text{x}}^{2}}}=\frac{1}{25}\int \frac{\text{dx}}{\frac{1}{25}-{{\text{x}}^{2}}}$

$=\frac{1}{25}\int \frac{\text{dx}}{{{\left( \frac{1}{5} \right)}^{2}}-{{\text{x}}^{2}}}=\frac{1}{25}\cdot \frac{1}{2\left( \frac{1}{5} \right)}\text{log}\left| \frac{\frac{1}{5}+\text{x}}{\frac{1}{5}-\text{x}} \right|+\text{c}=\frac{1}{10}\text{log}\left| \frac{1+5\text{x}}{1-5\text{x}} \right|+\text{c}$

(iii) I $=\int \frac{\text{dx}}{9{{\text{x}}^{2}}-1}=\frac{1}{9}\int \frac{\text{dx}}{{{\text{x}}^{2}}-\frac{1}{9}}=\frac{1}{9}\int \frac{\text{dx}}{{{\text{x}}^{2}}-{{\left( \frac{1}{3} \right)}^{2}}}$

$=\frac{1}{9}\frac{1}{2\left( \frac{1}{3} \right)}\text{log}\left| \frac{\text{x}-\frac{1}{3}}{\text{x}+\frac{1}{3}} \right|+\text{c}=\frac{1}{6}\text{log}\left| \frac{3\text{x}-1}{3\text{x}+1} \right|+\text{c}$

Q) Evaluate : 
(i) $\int \frac{3x}{1+2{{x}^{4}}}dx$ 
(ii) $\int \frac{{{2}^{\text{x}}}}{1-{{4}^{\text{x}}}}\text{dx}$ 
(iii) $\int \frac{2{{\text{x}}^{3}}}{4+{{\text{x}}^{8}}}dx$ 

Solution: 

(i) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\int \frac{3\text{x}}{1+2{{\text{x}}^{4}}}d\text{x}$

Putting ${{x}^{2}}=t\Rightarrow 2xdx=dt\Rightarrow dx=\frac{1}{2}dt$

$I=\frac{3}{2}\int \frac{dt}{1+2{{t}^{2}}}=\frac{3}{2}\cdot \frac{1}{2}\int \frac{dt}{\frac{1}{2}+{{t}^{2}}}=\frac{3}{4}\int \frac{dt}{{{(1/\sqrt{2})}^{2}}+{{t}^{2}}}$

$=\frac{3}{4}\cdot \frac{1}{1/\sqrt{2}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{\text{t}}{1/\sqrt{2}}+\text{c}=\frac{3}{2\sqrt{2}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \sqrt{2}\text{t} \right)+\text{c}=\frac{3}{2\sqrt{2}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \sqrt{2}{{\text{x}}^{2}} \right)+\text{c}$

(ii) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ I}=\int \frac{{{2}^{\text{x}}}}{1-{{4}^{\text{x}}}}\text{dx}=\int \frac{{{2}^{\text{x}}}}{1-{{\left( {{2}^{2}} \right)}^{\text{x}}}}\text{dx}=\int \frac{{{2}^{\text{x}}}}{1-{{\left( {{2}^{\text{x}}} \right)}^{2}}}\text{dx}$

Putting ${{2}^{\text{x}}}=\text{t}\Rightarrow {{2}^{\text{x}}}\text{log}2\text{dx}=\text{dt}\Rightarrow {{2}^{\text{x}}}\text{dx}=\frac{\text{dt}}{\text{log}2}$

$I=\frac{1}{\text{log}2}\int \frac{dt}{1-{{t}^{2}}}=\frac{1}{2\text{log}2}\text{log}\left| \frac{1+t}{1-t} \right|+c=\frac{1}{2\text{log}2}\text{log}\left| \frac{1+{{2}^{x}}}{1-{{2}^{x}}} \right|+c$

(iii) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ I}=\int \frac{2{{\text{x}}^{3}}}{4+{{\text{x}}^{8}}}\text{dx}$

Putting ${{\text{x}}^{4}}=\text{t}\Rightarrow 4{{\text{x}}^{3}}\text{dx}=\text{dt}\Rightarrow 2{{\text{x}}^{3}}\text{dx}=\frac{1}{2}\text{dt}$

I $~=\frac{1}{2}\int ~\frac{\text{dt}}{4+{{\text{t}}^{2}}}=\frac{1}{2}\int ~\frac{\text{dt}}{{{2}^{2}}+{{\text{t}}^{2}}}$

$~=\frac{1}{2}\cdot \frac{1}{2}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{\text{t}}{2}+\text{c}=\frac{1}{4}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{{{\text{x}}^{4}}}{2}+\text{c}$
